headlines.title: Saudi Arabia Set to Slash Oil Prices as Hormuz Reopens
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia is expected to slash the official selling prices of its crude loading for Asia in August, as Middle East’s crude benchmarks crashed amid the tentative reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the oil supply increase from the region. headlines.title: China’s Crude Imports Set to Hit Weakest Level Since 2016
headlines.summary: Chinese crude oil imports this month are on course to book an even weaker month than May, according to Kpler data, which sees the daily average at just 6.4 million barrels, as cited by Bloomberg. headlines.title: Qatar Offers First Crude Loadings to Buyers Since War Began
headlines.summary: Qatar is tendering crude for loading outside the Strait of Hormuz in July and August, in what is likely the first Qatari crude offering to buyers since the Iran war began on February 28, trade sources told Reuters on Friday. headlines.title: Qatar's LNG Recovery Picks Up Speed as Tankers Mass at Ras Laffan
headlines.summary: As many as eight empty LNG carriers have arrived at the Ras Laffan hub in Qatar and are set to load on superchilled gas in the coming days, Bloomberg has reported. headlines.title: Oil Heads for Weekly Loss as Hormuz Tanker Traffic Rebounds
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices were on course for a sharp weekly loss amid multiple reports about a strong rebound in tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Colombia's Cocaine Trade Now Outearns Its Oil Exports
headlines.summary: Drug lords in Colombia are making more money from cocaine than the government is making from crude oil sales, a report from a Colombian university has found.
